dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal requires counting consecutive bars above/below SMA (5+ bars confirmation). The allowed operators do not include a 'consecutive bars' counter, so this condition is simplified to a single close crossing the sma_50. The ADX confirmation threshold of 25 and trend strength filter (ADX > 20 exit) are directly translatable. The rule handles both long entries (uptrend) and short entries (downtrend) via CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal requires counting consecutive bars above/below SMA (5+ bars confirmation). The allowed operators do not include a 'consecutive bars' counter, so this condition is simplified to a single close crossing the sma_50. The ADX confirmation threshold of 25 and trend strength filter (ADX > 20 exit) are directly translatable. The rule handles both long entries (uptrend) and short entries (downtrend) via CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction.

source_rule_text: A trader should follow the price trend (buy in uptrends, sell short in downtrends) because market structure requires trend-following speculators to fulfill commercial hedger imbalances, and the market compensates this behavior through trend persistence.
